log.key: recommendations for fiscal year 2026-27 community development block grant funding allocations and review of the draft fiscal year 2026-27 an
log.title: Recommendations for Fiscal Year 2026-27 Community Development Block Grant Funding Allocations and Review of the Draft Fiscal Year 2026-27 Annual Action Plan. – Action – 45 minutes Presentation
log.draft: The Human Relations Commission reviews draft community development block grant funding recommendations.
log.verdict: checker rejected: The headline uses 'reviews draft' which implies a descriptive observation, but the official item specifies this is an 'Action' item about 'Recommendations for...funding allocations,' indicating decision-making authority, not merely review. The headline undersells the active decision-making nature of the agenda item and could mislead residents about the scope of the Commission's role.

The headline should reflect this critical distinction to be grounded in the official text.

log.key: approval of second amendment to the lease between the city of palo alto and community skating, inc. for the tennis facility at 3005 middlefi
log.title: Approval of Second Amendment to the Lease Between the City of Palo Alto and Community Skating, Inc. for the Tennis Facility at 3005 Middlefield Road, Extending the Term to January 1, 2040; CEQA Status – Categorically Exempt
log.draft: The City Council reviews an extension of the lease for the tennis facility at 3005 Middlefield Road.
log.verdict: checker rejected: The headline omits a material fact from the official item: the lessee is 'Community Skating, Inc.' The facility is described as a 'Tennis Facility,' but the lessee name suggests skating operations. This omission could mislead readers about what entity operates the facility and what activity occurs there. A grounded headline must include both the lessee identity and the facility type as presented in the official text.

log.key: second reading: ordinance of the council of the city of palo alto amending chapter 9.73 (city policy against arbitrary discrimination) of ti
log.title: SECOND READING: Ordinance of the Council of the City of Palo Alto Amending Chapter 9.73 (City Policy Against Arbitrary Discrimination) of Title 9 (Public Peace, Morals and Safety) of the Palo Alto Municipal Code to Add Sections 9.73.050 (Local CEDAW Principles) and 9.73.060 (Private Right of Action) and Amend Associated Sections (FIRST READING: March 23, 2026, PASSED: 7-0)
log.draft: The City Council votes on adding gender equity principles and a private right of action to the anti-discrimination policy.

log.key: second reading: ordinance of the council of the city of palo alto creating new pamc chapter 9.05 (entertainment zones) and establishing an e
log.title: SECOND READING: Ordinance of the Council of the City of Palo Alto Creating New PAMC Chapter 9.05 (Entertainment Zones) and Establishing an Entertainment Zone on a Portion of California Avenue (FIRST READING: April 20, 2026; PASSED: 7-0)
log.draft: The City Council approves an ordinance creating an entertainment zone on California Avenue.
log.verdict: checker rejected: The headline uses 'approves' which implies a completed action and positive outcome. The official item states this is 'SECOND READING' of an ordinance that 'PASSED' on first reading (April 20, 2026), but the current agenda item is for second reading — the approval is not yet finalized. The headline misrepresents the current procedural stage by suggesting the ordinance has been approved when it is still pending second reading.

log.key: citywide internal support and administration (11:15 am – 12:00 pm) staff presentation a) city attorney’s office (o:147-156) b) city auditor’
log.title: Citywide Internal Support and Administration (11:15 AM – 12:00 PM) Staff Presentation a) City Attorney’s Office (O:147-156) b) City Auditor’s Office (O:157-162) c) City Clerk’s Office (O:163-173) d) City Manager’s Office (Including Non-Profit Workplan Phase 1 Grants) (O:179-193) e) City Council (O:175-178) f) Non-Departmental (O:517-523)
log.draft: The Finance Committee reviews budgets for city departments and offices.
log.verdict: checker rejected: The headline claims the Finance Committee 'reviews budgets for city departments and offices,' but the official agenda item only shows these departments are presenting during a specific time block. The agenda text does not explicitly state that budget review occurs, only that there are staff presentations with associated document reference numbers. The headline adds an interpretive claim about the committee's action ('reviews budgets') that is not directly supported by the agenda item text provided.

log.verdict: checker rejected: The headline says 'votes on' which implies a decision outcome that has not occurred yet. The official item describes an agenda item scheduled for a specific time slot, not a completed vote. This adds interpretive language not directly supported by the source text.

log.verdict: checker rejected: The headline states the Council 'considers' a proclamation, but the official agenda item only confirms that a proclamation honoring June 2026 as LGBTQIA+ Pride Month has been added to the agenda. The word 'considers' implies deliberation or decision-making that has not yet occurred and is not established fact from the item text. The grounded requirement demands only facts directly supported by the official text.

log.key: public hearing/quasi-judicial. 762 san antonio road [24pln‐00120]: request for recommendation on a major architectural review application to
log.title: PUBLIC HEARING/QUASI-JUDICIAL. 762 San Antonio Road [24PLN‐00120]: Request for Recommendation on a Major Architectural Review Application to Demolish Three Existing Commercial Buildings and Construct an Eight-Story Multi-Family Residential Building Containing 228 Rental Apartments. Thirteen (13) Percent of the Units (30 Units) Would be Deed Restricted to Serve Lower Income Households. The Project is Proposed in Accordance with California Government Code Section 65589.5(d)(5) “Builders Remedy". A Senate Bill 330 Pre-Application was Filed January 9, 2024. CEQA Status: An Addendum to the Previously Certified Environmental Impact Report for the Housing Incentive Program Expansion and 788 San Antonio Mixed Use Project (SCH # 2019090070) has Been Prepared. Zoning District: CS (AD); Service Commercial (Automobile Dealership). Supplemental Report Added
log.draft: The Architectural Review Board reviews a proposal to demolish three buildings and construct an eight-story residential building with 228 apartments at 762 San Antonio Road.

log.title: PUBLIC HEARING & PROPOSITION 218 HEARING: Finance Committee Recommends Adoption of Seven Resolutions: 1) Approving the Fiscal Year 2027 Water Utility Financial Forecast, Reserve Management Practices and Reserve Transfer, and Amending Water Rate Schedules W-1, W-2, W-3, W-4, and W-7; 2) Approving the Fiscal Year 2027 Wastewater Collection Utility Financial Forecast and Reserve Management Practices, and Amending Wastewater Collection Rate Schedules S-1, S-2, S-6 and S-7; 3) Amending Refuse Utility Rate Schedules R-1 and R-C; 4) Approving the Fiscal Year 2027 Electric Utility Financial Forecast and Reserve Transfer, Amending the Electric Reserve Management Practices and Electric Rate Schedules E-1, E-1 TOU, E-2, E-2-G, E-4, E-4-G, E-4 TOU, E-7, E-7-G, E-7 TOU, E-14, E-16, E-EEC-1, and E-NSE-1, and Directing the Use of Up To $225,000 in Renewable Energy Certificate Exchange Net Revenues in Fiscal Year 2027 to Reduce Electric Customers’ Photovoltaic Generation and Energy Storage System Installation Costs; 5) Approving the Fiscal Year 2027 Gas Utility Financial Forecast, Reserve Transfer, and General Fund Transfer, and Amending the Reserve Management Practices and Gas Rate Schedules G-1, G-2, and G-3; 6) Amending Utility Rate Schedules EDF-1, and EDF-2; 7) Amending Storm Water Utility Rate Schedule D-1; CEQA Status: Not a project under CEQA Guidelines Sections 15378(b)(4) and (5) and Exempt Under Section 15273(a) Staff Presentation

log.title: QUASI-JUDICIAL. 3200 Park/340 Portage: Final Map and Street Naming [26PLN-00009]: Request for a Final Map to Subdivide One Existing 3.92-Acre Parcel for Condominium Purposes, Consistent with the Approved Development Agreement, and to Name the New Private Streets. Zone District: PC 5597. CEQA Status: Council Adopted Resolution 10123 Certifying the EIR for the Project on September 12, 2023 (SCH # 2021120444). Item Removed Off Agenda
log.draft: The City Council reviews a final map to subdivide a 3.92-acre parcel for condominiums.
log.verdict: checker rejected: The headline omits a critical fact: 'Item Removed Off Agenda.' A civic information site must convey that this item did not proceed to review, as stated in the official text. The headline's present-tense framing ('reviews') misrepresents the actual status and could mislead readers about whether action occurred.
